name: dragonslayer
summary: This is an experimental tool to test WPA3's SAE and EAP-pwd implementations for vulnerabilities. We also strongly recommend to perform code inspections to assure all vulnerabilities have been properly addressed.

documentation.readme: This is an experimental tool to test WPA3's SAE and EAP-pwd implementations for vulnerabilities. We also strongly recommend to perform code inspections to assure all vulnerabilities have been properly addressed.
usage_sections: a 0: perform reflection attack against either WPA3's SAE or EAP-pwd.
a 1: perform invalid curve attack against EAP-pwd.
a 2: perform second variant of the invalid curve attack against EAP-pwd. This variant is highly experimental.
a 3: perform zero-scalar attack against WPA3's SAE.
The wireless interface doesn't have to be set to monitor mode. This is the case for all our attack tools.

arguments.name: --no-wait
arguments.description: do not wait for tag to be removed before exiting

arguments.name: --only-one
arguments.description: run only one operation and exit

arguments.name: --success
arguments.description: success file for writing success update

arguments.name: --summary
arguments.description: summary file for writing status updates

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --interface
arguments.description: Network interface to bind to (e.g. wlan0mon, eth0).

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --verbose
arguments.description: Increase log verbosity (repeatable for higher levels).

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --proxy
arguments.description: HTTP or SOCKS proxy URL for outbound requests.

function_signatures.name: configure
function_signatures.description: Configure the tool with runtime options (interface, target, etc.) before execution.

function_signatures.name: scan
function_signatures.description: Perform a read-only scan / probe of the target.

function_signatures.name: exploit
function_signatures.description: Run the offensive step against the target. Returns ok, data, error envelope.

function_signatures.name: report
function_signatures.description: Render the result into a human-readable or machine-readable report.

function_signatures.name: validate
function_signatures.description: Pre-flight check: confirm the target is reachable and the tool can run.

function_signatures.name: cleanup
function_signatures.description: Remove any artifacts left behind by the tool (pcaps, sessions, files).

function_signatures.name: chain
function_signatures.description: Take a previous tool's result and feed it as input to this tool.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: apt-get install libnl-3-dev libnl-genl-3-dev pkg-config libssl-dev net-tools git libdbus-1-dev. This is an experimental tool to test WPA3's SAE and EAP-pwd implementations for vulnerabilities. We also strongly recommend to perform code inspections to assure all vulnerabilities have been properly addressed.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [wifi_2_4_ghz, wifi_5_ghz, shell_linux] in the Wireless category. Fits when the engagement needs: This is an experimental tool to test WPA3's SAE and EAP-pwd implementations for vulnerabilities. We also strongly recommend to perform code inspections to assure all vulnerabilities have been properly addressed.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: This is an experimental tool to test WPA3's SAE and EAP-pwd implementations for vulnerabilities. We also strongly recommend to perform code inspections to assure all vulnerabilities have been properly addressed.
